channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Drones are changing the face of warfare, including battlefield medicine
item.description: There have been hundreds of thousands of casualties on both sides of the war in Ukraine, and by some estimates more than 80% are now caused by drones. It's changed the nature of battlefield medicine.
item.content:encoded: There have been hundreds of thousands of casualties on both sides of the war in Ukraine, and by some estimates more than 80% are now caused by drones. It's changed the nature of battlefield medicine.
item.dc:creator: Jay Price

item.title: Asia defense summit opens amid doubts over U.S. priorities
item.description: The Shangri-La Dialogue, hosted by the International Institute for Strategic Studies, will also address tensions in the Middle East and Russia's war on Ukraine.
item.content:encoded:
The Shangri-La Dialogue, hosted by the International Institute for Strategic Studies, will also address tensions in the Middle East and Russia's war on Ukraine.

(Image credit: Achmad Ibrahim)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Israel, Lebanon officials to meet. And, judge won't block Trump's mail-in voting order
item.description: Israel and Lebanon officials are set to meet today as U.S.-Iran peace talks continue. And, a federal judge declined to block Trump's executive order restricting mail-in voting.
item.content:encoded:
Israel and Lebanon officials are set to meet today as U.S.-Iran peace talks continue. And, a federal judge declined to block Trump's executive order restricting mail-in voting.

(Image credit: Kawant Haju)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Russian drone launched against Ukraine crashes in Romania, injuring 2
item.description: A Russian drone that was part of an overnight attack on Ukraine crashed into an apartment building in eastern Romania, injuring two people, Romanian authorities said Friday.
item.content:encoded:
A Russian drone that was part of an overnight attack on Ukraine crashed into an apartment building in eastern Romania, injuring two people, Romanian authorities said Friday.

(Image credit: via AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: WHO chief lands in Congo to address rare Ebola outbreak amid distrust and insecurity
item.description: The head of the World Health Organization arrived in Congo's capital, Kinshasa, to witness efforts against an outbreak of a rare type of Ebola virus.
item.content:encoded:
The head of the World Health Organization arrived in Congo's capital, Kinshasa, to witness efforts against an outbreak of a rare type of Ebola virus.

(Image credit: Samy Ntumba Shambuyi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Arsenal's glory weekend beckons, and soccer fans all over the world are watching
item.description: This weekend is Arsenal F.C.'s victory parade. Fans from London to Lagos are celebrating a dream season — and watching to see if one more trophy is on the way.
item.content:encoded:
This weekend is Arsenal F.C.'s victory parade. Fans from London to Lagos are celebrating a dream season — and watching to see if one more trophy is on the way.

(Image credit: Julian Finney)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: U.S. and Iran trade strikes. How will this affect deal to end war?
item.description: Do new strikes affect a potential deal to end the war with Iran? NPR's Scott Detrow breaks it down with NPR International Correspondent Aya Batrawy and NPR Pentagon Correspondent Tom Bowman.
item.content:encoded: Do new strikes affect a potential deal to end the war with Iran? NPR's Scott Detrow breaks it down with NPR International Correspondent Aya Batrawy and NPR Pentagon Correspondent Tom Bowman.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: The possible return of Somali pirates poses a new problem for the shipping industry
item.description: As the global economy focuses on the Strait of Hormuz crisis, another shipping crisis looms in the region — the return of Somali pirates.
item.content:encoded: As the global economy focuses on the Strait of Hormuz crisis, another shipping crisis looms in the region — the return of Somali pirates.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Australian woman charged over travel to Syria to join Islamic State
item.description: The arrest came eight months after she returned to Australia with her children from Lebanon.
item.content:encoded:
The arrest came eight months after she returned to Australia with her children from Lebanon.

(Image credit: Baderkhan Ahmad)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Orthodox Jewish women in Israel may now take a rabbinic exam, like men
item.description: Until now, only Orthodox Jewish men in Israel have been allowed to take exams to become rabbis. After a long campaign, women can now also take the test - but still can't become rabbis
item.content:encoded:
Until now, only Orthodox Jewish men in Israel have been allowed to take exams to become rabbis. After a long campaign, women can now also take the test - but still can't become rabbis

(Image credit: Ofir Berman for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin
